meta.name: Chili-Citrus Rice
meta.subtitle: Fluffy rice with lime/lemon, garlic, and a gentle chili warmth

summary: A bright, savory rice side finished with citrus zest/juice and chili. Pairs with chili-lime chicken, citrus fish, orange-chili pork, shrimp, and roasted vegetables for easy bowls.

yield.notes: Makes ~6 cups cooked rice (varies by rice type).

optional.label: Rice cooker
optional.notes: Use the rice cooker method variation for set-and-forget cooking.

ingredients.label: Long-grain white rice (uncooked)
ingredients.notes: Jasmine or basmati work best. See variations for brown rice.

ingredients.label: Water (or light broth)
ingredients.notes: For most long-grain white rice: 2 cups rice : 2 cups liquid. Adjust to your rice brand if needed.

ingredients.label: Olive oil or butter (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds silkiness; omit butter for vegan.

ingredients.label: Red pepper flakes (or mild chili powder)
ingredients.notes: Use 1/4 tsp for very mild; up to 1 tsp for more heat.

ingredients.label: Citrus zest (lime or lemon)
ingredients.notes: Zest before juicing. Lime = brighter; lemon = softer.

steps.title: Rinse rice (recommended for fluffy grains)
instructions: Rinse rice in a fine-mesh strainer under cool water until the water runs mostly clear (30–60 seconds).
checks: Rice looks less starchy and drains well

steps.title: Build the pot
instructions: Add rice, water (or broth), salt, and oil/butter (optional) to a saucepan.
Stir in the minced garlic and chili flakes (or chili powder).
Bring to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
checks: Garlic and chili are evenly dispersed in the liquid

steps.title: Simmer, cover, and steam
instructions: Once boiling, reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15 minutes (avoid lifting the lid).
Turn off heat and let rice rest, covered, for 5 minutes to finish steaming.
checks: Rice is tender and liquid is absorbed

steps.title: Finish with citrus and fluff
instructions: In a small bowl, combine citrus zest and citrus juice.
Uncover rice and fluff with a fork.
Drizzle citrus mixture over the rice and gently fold to distribute.
Taste and adjust: add a pinch of salt, more zest, or a tiny squeeze of juice if needed.
checks: Citrus is bright but not sour (use more zest vs more juice if you want more aroma without extra acidity)

refrigerator.notes: Cool quickly; store airtight. Great for bowls and quick stir-fries.

freezer.notes: Freeze flat in portion bags for quick reheat.

reheat.notes: Add 1–2 tsp water per cup before reheating to restore fluff. Add a fresh squeeze of citrus after reheating if desired.

handling: Cool rice promptly (within 1 hour) and refrigerate to reduce food safety risk.

variations.label: Rice cooker method
notes: Add rice, water/broth, salt, garlic, and chili to the rice cooker.
Cook on the standard white rice setting.
Fold in citrus zest + juice after cooking and fluffing.

variations.label: Brown rice version
notes: Use 2 cups brown rice + 2.5 cups water/broth (600 ml) (adjust per brand).

variations.label: Coconut chili-citrus rice
notes: Replace 1/2 cup (120 ml) of the water with coconut milk for a richer, Caribbean-leaning side.
Keep citrus finish; consider using lime.

variations.label: Herby chili-citrus rice
notes: Fold in 1/4 cup chopped cilantro or parsley with the citrus finish.

weeknightNotes: Make a double batch for bowls all week.
Finish with zest + juice after cooking for the cleanest citrus flavor.
